sources.title: Antigua and Barbuda CIU — Investment options
Antigua and Barbuda Citizenship by Investment Act
Antigua and Barbuda Citizenship by Investment Unit
Antigua and Barbuda Immigration — Citizenship FAQs

review.note: Reviewed against the Afghan constitutional and nationality-law framework. Descent is the dominant route; dual citizenship is not officially recognized. Administration of naturalization is operationally uncertain under the current de facto authorities. No CBI.
Parent Afghan national; not unrestricted jus soli.

routes.title: Afghan citizenship through an Afghan parent
routes.summary: A child with at least one Afghan citizen parent is an Afghan citizen (jus sanguinis), regardless of birthplace; paternal transmission is clearly established, maternal transmission less certain in practice.

variants.label: Afghan citizenship through an Afghan parent

routes.title: Naturalization after about five years residence
routes.summary: The nationality law provides for naturalization after roughly five years of lawful residence plus integration and Ministry of Interior approval; grant is discretionary.

variants.label: Naturalization after about five years residence

timeline.note: Five-year figure from secondary sources; current administration of naturalization under the de facto authorities is uncertain and requires verification.

routes.title: Citizenship at birth through an Afghan parent
routes.summary: Birth to an Afghan parent creates nationality. Birth in Afghanistan alone to foreign parents is not unrestricted jus soli; territorial rules cover foundlings and otherwise-stateless children.

variants.label: Citizenship at birth through an Afghan parent

review.note: Reviewed against Albanian constitutional nationality framework. Ordinary naturalization commonly about five years residence. No CBI.
Parent Albania national; not unrestricted jus soli.

routes.title: Albania nationality through a Albania parent
routes.summary: A child of a Albania parent is a national under the constitutional nationality framework and nationality statute.

variants.label: Albania nationality through a Albania parent

routes.title: Naturalization after about 5 years residence
routes.summary: Adults generally need about 5 years lawful residence before ordinary naturalization, plus character, language or integration, and other statutory conditions. Grant is discretionary under the nationality law.

variants.label: Naturalization after about 5 years residence

timeline.note: Residence floor modeled from the ordinary naturalization track in comparative nationality sources against the constitutional framework; case-check the current nationality statute for reductions and renunciation rules.

routes.title: Citizenship at birth through a Albania parent
routes.summary: Birth to a Albania parent creates nationality. Birth in Albania alone to two foreign parents is not unrestricted jus soli under the modeled framework.

variants.label: Citizenship at birth through a Albania parent

routes.title: Naturalization as the spouse of a citizen
routes.summary: A spouse may naturalize after three years of marriage and one year of residence, exempt from the seven-year bar.

variants.label: Naturalization as the spouse of a citizen

timeline.note: The one-year residence reading is the stronger one; some secondary sources cite three years.

review.note: Reviewed against the Algerian Nationality Code. No CBI.
Parent Algerian; not unrestricted jus soli.

routes.title: Algerian nationality through an Algerian parent
routes.summary: A child of an Algerian parent is Algerian under the Nationality Code.

variants.label: Algerian nationality through an Algerian parent

routes.title: Naturalization after seven years residence
routes.summary: Adults generally need seven years residence in Algeria, assimilation into Algerian society, livelihood, good character, and majority. Naturalization is by decree and discretionary.

variants.label: Naturalization after seven years residence

routes.title: Citizenship at birth through an Algerian parent
routes.summary: Birth to an Algerian parent creates Algerian nationality. Birth in Algeria alone to two foreign parents is not unrestricted jus soli.

variants.label: Citizenship at birth through an Algerian parent

routes.title: Naturalization as the spouse of a citizen
routes.summary: A spouse of an Algerian may naturalize after two years of residence (vs seven).

variants.label: Naturalization as the spouse of a citizen

timeline.note: Ordinance 70-86 art. 9 bis; gender-neutral since the 2005 reform.

review.note: Reviewed against Andorran nationality framework. Long residence path; renunciation of prior nationality required. No CBI.
Parent Andorran; not general jus soli.
Residence by investment products are not citizenship.

routes.title: Andorran nationality through an Andorran parent
routes.summary: A child of an Andorran parent is Andorran under nationality law, subject to registration rules for birth abroad.

variants.label: Andorran nationality through an Andorran parent

routes.title: Naturalization after long residence
routes.summary: Ordinary naturalization generally requires about twenty years permanent residence (ten if educated in Andorra), integration, and renunciation of prior nationality. Grant is discretionary.

variants.label: Naturalization after long residence

routes.title: Citizenship at birth through an Andorran parent
routes.summary: Birth to an Andorran parent creates Andorran nationality. Birth in Andorra alone to two foreign parents is not general jus soli.

variants.label: Citizenship at birth through an Andorran parent

review.note: Reviewed against Angolan constitutional nationality framework. Ordinary naturalization after long residence. No CBI.
Parent Angolan; not unrestricted jus soli.

routes.title: Angolan nationality through an Angolan parent
routes.summary: A child of an Angolan parent is Angolan under nationality law.

variants.label: Angolan nationality through an Angolan parent

routes.title: Naturalization after ten years residence
routes.summary: Adults generally need about ten years habitual and regular residence, Portuguese language and civic knowledge, livelihood, and good character. Naturalization is discretionary.

variants.label: Naturalization after ten years residence

routes.title: Citizenship at birth through an Angolan parent
routes.summary: Birth to an Angolan parent creates Angolan nationality. Birth in Angola alone to two foreign parents is not unrestricted jus soli.

variants.label: Citizenship at birth through an Angolan parent

routes.title: Naturalization as the spouse of a citizen
routes.summary: A spouse may acquire nationality where the union predates the request by at least five years.

variants.label: Naturalization as the spouse of a citizen

timeline.note: Lei 2/2016; spousal consent plus character and integration criteria.

review.note: All acquisition modes reviewed against the Constitution, Citizenship Act and current Citizenship by Investment Unit options.

routes.title: Citizenship through a citizen parent
routes.summary: A person born abroad acquires citizenship at birth through a qualifying Antigua and Barbuda citizen parent under section 113 of the Constitution.

variants.label: Citizenship through a citizen parent

routes.title: Naturalization after a qualifying residence period
routes.summary: The Citizenship Act requires residence throughout the twelve months immediately before application and at least five aggregate years during the preceding seven-year period.

variants.label: Naturalization after a qualifying residence period

timeline.note: The eight-year window contains a continuous final year plus five aggregate years in the preceding seven; it is not an eight-year continuous-residence rule.

routes.title: Citizenship by birth in Antigua and Barbuda
routes.summary: A person born in Antigua and Barbuda acquires citizenship at birth, subject to the Constitution’s diplomatic-immunity and hostile-occupation exceptions.

variants.label: Citizenship by birth in Antigua and Barbuda

routes.title: Citizenship by Investment Programme
routes.summary: Government-administered direct citizenship programme with contribution, real-estate and other approved investment options.

variants.label: Citizenship by Investment Programme

timeline.note: The National Development Fund option currently starts at US$230,000. Other approved routes and fees differ, and the principal applicant must spend at least five days in Antigua and Barbuda during the first five calendar years.

routes.title: Naturalization as the spouse of a citizen
routes.summary: A spouse married to and living with a national for three years may register as a citizen.

variants.label: Naturalization as the spouse of a citizen
